headword_jp: {非|ひ}{手|て}{番|ばん}プレイヤー

definitions.gloss_en: the player(s) whose turn it is not; the non-active or reactive player
definitions.gloss_jp: {手|て}{番|ばん}ではないプレイヤー；{相|あい}{手|て}{方|がた}のプレイヤー
definitions.explanation_en: Designates the player(s) who must wait while the active player acts. May have reactive actions available (e.g. interrupts, saves) depending on the ruleset.
definitions.implicit_notes: 非 (non-) is a formal negation prefix; the compound is a logical antonym formed from 非 + 手番プレイヤー. Not specific to any game system.
definitions.orthography_notes: Uses the classical negation prefix 非 (hi-). The compound is not attested as a standalone dictionary entry but is constructed from standard elements and cited as a standard form in Wikipedia Japan (ターン (ゲーム)).
definitions.game_setting_notes: Used in rulebooks that explicitly name both roles. In two-player miniature wargames, refers to the opponent. In multiplayer games, refers to all players except the active player.

usage_jp: {非|ひ}{手|て}{番|ばん}プレイヤーは{反|はん}{応|のう}できる (the non-active player may react)
usage_en: "reactive player", "non-active player", or "your opponent" depending on context.
comparative_notes: The 非手番 prefix construction is productive in formal Japanese but uncommon in casual speech. Rulebooks use it for precision when contrasting active and reactive roles.

examples.japanese: {非|ひ}{手|て}{番|ばん}プレイヤーはこの{段|だん}{階|かい}に{反|はん}{応|のう}できない。
examples.english: The non-active player cannot respond during this phase.

cross_references.note: 手番プレイヤー — the active player
